text:
```json
[
  "Computers cannot process text; they can only process numbers.",
  "Vectorization is the process of converting words into numbers.",
  "Vectorization converts input data from text format into vectors of real numbers supported by ML models.",
  "Bag of Words (BoW) converts text into numerical form by representing a document as a vector of word counts.",
  "BoW vectorization involves three steps: tokenization to extract unique words, building vocabulary with assigned indexes, and vectorization by counting word occurrences.",
  "TF-IDF is a statistical method that evaluates the importance of a word in a document relative to a collection.",
  "TF-IDF adjusts word weight based on how frequently a word appears across multiple documents.",
  "TF (Term Frequency) measures how often a term appears in a document.",
  "IDF (Inverse Document Frequency) reduces the weight of common words and increases the weight of rare words.",
  "TF-IDF equals TF multiplied by IDF.",
  "TF-IDF assigns higher weights to words that are both frequent within a document and rare across the entire collection.",
  "Word2Vec represents words as dense vectors in a high-dimensional space, typically 100-300 dimensions.",
  "Word2Vec is based on the idea that words occurring in similar contexts tend to have similar meanings.",
  "Word2Vec has two architectures: CBOW predicts the target word from surrounding context, and Skip-gram predicts surrounding context words given the target word.",
  "Word2Vec generates static embeddings where the same vector represents a word regardless of context.",
  "GloVe is an unsupervised learning algorithm developed by Stanford for obtaining vector representations of words.",
  "GloVe captures global word co-occurrence statistics from the entire corpus by constructing a word co-occurrence matrix.",
  "FastText, developed by Facebook AI, incorporates subword information making it effective for morphologically rich languages.",
  "FastText represents words as bags of character n-grams.",
  "FastText can generate vectors for out-of-vocabulary (OOV) words.",
  "Unlike static embeddings, contextualized embeddings generate different representations for a word based on its context.",
  "Contextualized embeddings capture polysemy (multiple meanings of a word) more effectively.",
  "ELMo generates contextualized word embeddings using deep bidirectional LSTMs.",
  "ELMo captures context from both directions and generates word representations that change based on sentence context.",
  "Transformer models use self-attention mechanisms to process entire sequences at once.",
  "Transformer models capture long-range dependencies more effectively than sequential models like LSTMs.",
  "BERT learns word representations by looking at both left and right context.",
  "BERT uses only the encoder network to learn a representation of the input text."
]
```
json_data: Computers cannot process text; they can only process numbers.
Vectorization is the process of converting words into numbers.
Vectorization converts input data from text format into vectors of real numbers supported by ML models.
Bag of Words (BoW) converts text into numerical form by representing a document as a vector of word counts.
BoW vectorization involves three steps: tokenization to extract unique words, building vocabulary with assigned indexes, and vectorization by counting word occurrences.
TF-IDF is a statistical method that evaluates the importance of a word in a document relative to a collection.
TF-IDF adjusts word weight based on how frequently a word appears across multiple documents.
TF (Term Frequency) measures how often a term appears in a document.
IDF (Inverse Document Frequency) reduces the weight of common words and increases the weight of rare words.
TF-IDF equals TF multiplied by IDF.
TF-IDF assigns higher weights to words that are both frequent within a document and rare across the entire collection.
Word2Vec represents words as dense vectors in a high-dimensional space, typically 100-300 dimensions.
Word2Vec is based on the idea that words occurring in similar contexts tend to have similar meanings.
Word2Vec has two architectures: CBOW predicts the target word from surrounding context, and Skip-gram predicts surrounding context words given the target word.
Word2Vec generates static embeddings where the same vector represents a word regardless of context.
GloVe is an unsupervised learning algorithm developed by Stanford for obtaining vector representations of words.
GloVe captures global word co-occurrence statistics from the entire corpus by constructing a word co-occurrence matrix.
FastText, developed by Facebook AI, incorporates subword information making it effective for morphologically rich languages.
FastText represents words as bags of character n-grams.
FastText can generate vectors for out-of-vocabulary (OOV) words.
Unlike static embeddings, contextualized embeddings generate different representations for a word based on its context.
Contextualized embeddings capture polysemy (multiple meanings of a word) more effectively.
ELMo generates contextualized word embeddings using deep bidirectional LSTMs.
ELMo captures context from both directions and generates word representations that change based on sentence context.
Transformer models use self-attention mechanisms to process entire sequences at once.
Transformer models capture long-range dependencies more effectively than sequential models like LSTMs.
BERT learns word representations by looking at both left and right context.
BERT uses only the encoder network to learn a representation of the input text.
